content: 연희초등학교는 1970년대에 지어진 전형적인 공립 초등학교 건물이다. 낮고 어두운 복도, 교실마다 닫힌 문, 운동장을 등진 배치 — 이 학교의 공간은 수십 년 동안 아이들에게 무언가를 가르쳐왔다.
A wall can be a garden. Its surface, its shadow, its sound of rain — all are landscape.
The solution is a 2.8-meter wall in textured concrete, angled slightly inward at the top to deflect sound and direct rainwater into a narrow channel running along its base.
A rest shelter at the midpoint of a popular hiking trail on the southern face of Bukhansan. The program is simple: shade, seating, drinking water, and an emergency telephone.

image_caption: Shelter integrated into the rock face.

content: The structure leans against an existing granite outcrop, using the rock as its north wall. The roof is folded Corten steel, pre-weathered before installation.
The rock was already there. The building's job was to make it more itself.
A working studio and living space for a ceramicist in the Yeonhui neighborhood of northwestern Seoul.
Light from the north has no agenda. It does not flatter or deceive. It is simply reliable.
The studio is oriented north for stable working light. The kiln room sits at the back, ventilated by a tall brick chimney that also acts as the building's main vertical landmark.
The exterior is plain brick, local and inexpensive. Inside, the floors are poured concrete, sealed and polished.
